mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales indicate strengthening consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS YoY change on 20-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window or the signal generates negative mean return in expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A falling unemployment rate below 5.0% signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, lifting equity prices over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) in months where UNRATE first drops below 5.0%, or the signal's win rate over triggered instances is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the nominal federal funds rate is below the trailing 12-month CPI inflation rate, real interest rates are negative, encouraging risk-taking and leverage that lifts equity valuations over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < trailing 12-month CPI inflation (calculated from CPIAUCSL YoY change)
failure_condition: Rank IC of the real-rate condition on 60-day forward returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal fails to produce positive mean excess returns in negative real rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing activity and economic acceleration, which supports corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative mean 20-day returns in at least half of expansion triggers, or the hit rate of positive returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect strong residential investment and consumer sentiment, leading to higher economic growth and positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between HOUST YoY change and forward 60-day S&P 500 returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10), or the mean return in high-HOUST-growth periods is not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in both nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicate a reinforcing cycle of employment growth and consumer spending that strongly lifts equity prices over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM change > 0 AND RSAFS MoM change > 0
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return when both series rise is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or is lower than the unconditional average return over the same period.

mechanism_narrative: A perceivable increase in the federal funds rate above 0.25% signals a shift from ultra-accommodative policy, compressing equity valuations through higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal's mean 20-day forward return during triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) or the win rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A very low and still declining unemployment rate in a late-cycle environment indicates overheating labor markets that precede margin compression and eventual recession, generating negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The mean 60-day forward return following late-cycle labor tightness triggers is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or the signal produces positive returns in more than 40% of triggered instances.
